name: How to Add Products & Services to Google Business Profile
description: Find out how to add products & services to your Google Business Profile to attract customers and improve local SEO. Complete step-by-step guide inside!
main_title: Step-by-Step: Add Products & Services to Google Business
richtext:
Have you ever searched for a business and instantly found exactly what they offer? That’s the beauty of a well-optimized Google Business Profile (GBP).

When you add services and products to Google Business Profile, you’re making life easier for potential customers. They see what you sell, what you do, and why they should choose you—without digging through your website.

And here’s the thing: this also helps with local SEO. Google loves details. The more relevant info you provide, the better your chances of ranking higher in search results.

Are you ready to set up your GBP services and products the right way? Everything you need is in this guide. 

TL;DR: How to Add Products & Services to Google Business Profile

  • Adding products and services to GBP improves local rankings and customer engagement.
  • Use high-quality images (minimum 720x720 pixels) and clear, detailed descriptions.
  • Avoid generic content, low-quality images, and inconsistent business details.
  • Google prefers JPEG and PNG formats; avoid TIFF, BMP, and GIF files.
  • Need an easier way to manage your Google listings? Try Synup today.

What Are Products & Services on GBP?

If Google Business Profile (GBP) is your digital storefront, the Products and Services sections are like your display window. They show people what you offer before they even step in.

Products vs. Services: What’s the Difference?

  • Products: These let you showcase specific items you sell. Each product can include a name, description, price (optional), and even a photo. 
  • Services: These list what your business offers—whether it's plumbing, digital marketing, or personal training. Unlike products, services don’t have images, but they help customers understand what you do.

Why This Matters

People want quick answers. If they’re searching for a "home cleaning service" or "best cabin accommodation near me," a well-optimized Google Business Profile helps them decide fast.

Here’s why adding products and services changes the game for your local SEO:

  • Boosts local search rankings: Google prioritizes complete and detailed business profiles.
  • Helps customers make faster decisions: A clear list of what you offer reduces guesswork.
  • Adds a visual edge: High-quality product images make your listing pop in search results.

Essentially, if you add products or services to your GBP, you're giving Google (and potential customers) more reasons to choose you.

Google’s Guidelines

Google has rules on what you can and can't add. Avoid misleading claims, inappropriate content, or keyword stuffing. If in doubt, check out Google’s official guidelines.

Real-World Examples

Need some inspiration? Here’s how different businesses can use this feature:

  • Restaurant: List menu items under "Products" (e.g., "Classic Cheeseburger") and services like "Catering" under "Services."
  • Retail Store: Add featured products like "Wireless Earbuds" and product categories like "Smartphones."
  • Salon: Showcase "Hair Coloring" and "Bridal Makeup" under GBP services setup to attract bookings.

Step-by-Step Guide to Adding Products & Services to GBP

Now that you know why Google Business Profile products and services matter, let’s get into the how.

Adding them isn’t rocket science, but doing it the right way is where most businesses fall short. Every detail counts, from choosing the right images to picking the best file format. 

  1. Use High-Resolution Images

Google hates blurry, pixelated images. So do customers. Here’s what works best:

  • Minimum resolution: 720 x 720 pixels (Good enough, but not great.)
  • Ideal resolution: 1024 x 576 pixels (Perfect for cover photos and product displays.)
  • Max file size: Between 10 KB to 5 MB (Keeps loading times fast.)

Think of it this way: If someone searches for a "handmade leather wallet" and sees a crisp, professional-looking image, they’re way more likely to click. The same goes for services—if you run a salon, high-quality photos of your work can be the difference between a booking and a scroll-past.

  1. Best File Formats for Google Business Profile

Not all image files are the same. Google prefers certain formats because they load faster and display correctly across all devices. Here’s what works:

  • JPEG (JPG): Best for photos of products, storefronts, and interiors.
  • PNG: Ideal for logos, transparent images, or anything that needs sharp edges.

What to avoid?

  • TIFF, BMP, and GIF. They don’t render well in Google results, meaning your GBP services setup might look broken or weird on different screens.

Bottom line: High-quality images in the right format make your Google Business Profile stand out. Whether you’re listing products or adding services to GBP, visuals can make or break first impressions.

Common Mistakes (and How to Avoid Them)

Optimizing your Google Business Profile products and services is great, unless you do it wrong. Here’s what to avoid:

  1. Vague Content

“Best coffee in town” sounds nice, but it’s vague. Instead, describe what makes it special: “Organic Ethiopian roast with hints of caramel and dark chocolate.” The more details, the better.

  1. Don’t Upload Bad Images

Blurry, dark, or irrelevant images? Hard pass. Use high-quality, well-lit photos that showcase your actual products or services.

  1. Stay Consistent

Your GBP services setup should match your website and social profiles. Conflicting info confuses both customers and Google. It’s best to use the same terms for your product and services while listing them.

  1. Keep It Clean

Too many listing items are overwhelming. Focus on your top products and services for a clear, effective profile.

Also Read: Top 20 Google My Business Management Tools for Agencies

‍

Summing Up

Adding Google Business Profile products and services can improve visibility, boost local SEO, and help customers make faster decisions. Use clear descriptions, high-quality images, and consistent information. Want an even easier way to manage your GBP services setup? Synup helps businesses streamline listings, optimize visibility, and attract more customers.

‍

How to Add Products & Services to Google Business Profile: FAQs

How do I add a product category in Google My Business?

Log in to the Google Business Profile dashboard, navigate to the “Products” tab before clicking “Add Product.” Choose a category from Google’s list or create your own. Adding a category helps Google understand what you sell, increasing your chances of showing up in relevant searches.

How do I get my products to show up on Google?

Make sure your product descriptions are clear and relevant. Use Google Business Profile products with SEO-friendly keywords. High-quality images and competitive pricing also help. If your products aren’t appearing, check Google’s guidelines to ensure compliance.

How do I add a service area to Google My Business?

Navigate to your Business Profile and click “Edit Profile.” Under “Location and areas,” click the pencil icon next to “Service area.” Add suggested areas or enter them manually, then save. Updating your service area ensures your GBP services setup reaches the right customers.

‍
